the pen is mightier than sword  
the pen is mightier than board  
the pen is mightier than cord  

pizzelle lived in a quiet corner of the town where the streets were lined with sleepy shops and the only sounds were the occasional rustle of leaves and the distant chirping of birds. her donut shop, "pizzelle's sweet twist," was a small, pastel-colored building with a sign that swayed gently in the breeze. she was a young toon girl with a gentle smile and a heart full of kindness, but her shop was nearly always empty. despite this, she made dozens of donuts every day, just in case someone showed up. she believed in being prepared, in kindness, in the idea that maybe, just maybe, someone would need a warm, sugary treat.  

but the days passed, and the donuts piled up. they sat in boxes, in bins, in the corners of the shop, waiting for someone to take them. but no one came. pizzelle tried putting up signs, offering free samples, even leaving a few on the doorstep for passersby. nothing worked. the donuts were still there, and they were beginning to spoil. she didn’t want to throw them away. she didn’t want to waste them. so, she did what she thought was the only option left. she ate them. one by one, then two by two, until the boxes were empty and her heart was full. she didn’t mind the change. she didn’t mind the weight. she just wanted to make sure nothing went to waste.
